varieties.name: Mon Thong (Monthong, D159, Golden Pillow)
varieties.origin: Thailand, registered 1979, mid-season variety
varieties.traits: Large elongated oval-cylindrical fruit, 2-6kg, yellowish-brown husk with pronounced beak, lobed appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, full-bodied, creamy golden-yellow, mild sweet taste, moderate aroma, smaller seeds, custard-like texture
varieties.yield_notes: Medium to high, reliable producer, Thailand's #1 export variety (95% of Thai durian exports)

varieties.name: Chanee (D123, Gibbon)
varieties.origin: Thailand, early-season variety, named after gibbon
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit with distinctive yellow color, thick flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, sweet, buttery yellow flesh with hint of bitterness, sticky texture, not fibrous, sometimes alcoholic taste
varieties.yield_notes: High, consistent cropper, comprises 33% of Thailand's durian cultivation

varieties.name: Kan Yao (Kanyao, D158, Long Stem)
varieties.origin: Thailand, mid-season variety
varieties.traits: Less common variety, characterized by long fruit stem, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet and odorless when ripe, creamy texture, well-balanced flavor, less pungent than most varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Medium, comprises 5% of Thai cultivation
varieties.availability: Limited availability in Australia, occasionally from specialist tropical fruit nurseries

varieties.name: Gaan Yaow (Mid-Season Thai)
varieties.origin: Thailand, mid-season cultivar recommended for Queensland
varieties.traits: Well-balanced characteristics suitable for subtropical adaptation
varieties.flesh_fruit: Well-balanced flavor profile with less pungency, making it popular among durian newcomers
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Southeast Queensland conditions
varieties.availability: Growing availability in humid subtropical climates area specialty nurseries

varieties.name: D24 (Sultan, Bukit Merah)
varieties.origin: Malaysia, registered 1934, one of oldest commercial varieties, 80+ years cultivation history
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, homogeneous green husk color, distinct from Musang King, thorns in clusters
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, off-white to pale cream colored, big seeds, bittersweet taste, can become pungent and alcoholic when well-fermented
varieties.yield_notes: High when cross-pollinated, reliable mid-season variety

varieties.name: Kampung Durian (Australian Seedling Types)
varieties.origin: Far North Queensland, grown 30-40+ years from Southeast Asian seeds brought in 1970s-1980s, multiple generations
varieties.traits: Variable characteristics, 1-2.5kg fruits, open-pollinated diversity, adapted to Australian conditions over generations
varieties.flesh_fruit: Highly variable - some sweet, some creamy, some bittersweet, some pungent, some mild. Lucky dip quality but fresh Australian-grown
varieties.yield_notes: Variable, trees can be very productive once established (decades old)

varieties.name: Gun Pun, Gob, Mon Thong (NT Commercial)
varieties.origin: Northern Territory, Australia - commercial varieties established since 1988
varieties.traits: Commercially proven in Australian tropical conditions, adapted to NT climate challenges
varieties.flesh_fruit: Variable quality depending on variety and season, Gun Pun and Mon Thong most reliable
varieties.yield_notes: Proven commercial production - Tropical Primary Products (Siah family) has successfully produced crops since mid-1990s
varieties.availability: Primarily grown in NT for domestic market (Sydney, Melbourne, humid subtropical climates, Adelaide distribution), grafted material occasionally available

varieties.name: D99 (Kop Kecil, Gob Kecil, Frog)
varieties.origin: Malaysia/Thailand, early-season variety, Thai name "Kop" means frog
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, can produce two crops annually including off-season
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality, slightly less premium than D24, golden-yellow color
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - can bear twice yearly, good for extending season, self-compatible unlike most varieties
varieties.availability: Available from specialist tropical fruit nurseries in Queensland and NSW

varieties.name: Kradum Thong (Gradumtong, Golden Button)
varieties.origin: Thailand, early-season cultivar, comprises 2% of Thai cultivation
varieties.traits: Named for button-like appearance, early maturing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, creamy flesh, golden color, moderate aroma
varieties.yield_notes: Good, used as pollinizer in Thailand, important for cross-pollination

varieties.name: Monthong (Mon Thong, Golden Pillow)
varieties.origin: Thailand, now widely cultivated in tropical monsoon climates and Tamil Nadu since 1990s
varieties.traits: Large fruit 2-6kg, yellowish-brown husk, pronounced beak
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, creamy, mild sweet taste with moderate aroma, golden-yellow, smaller seeds, custard-like
varieties.yield_notes: High, reliable, most popular export variety globally

varieties.name: D24 (Sultan, Durian Sultan)
varieties.origin: Malaysia 1934, introduced to India 1970s-1980s, well-established
varieties.traits: Medium-large, homogeneous green husk, clustered thorns
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick golden-yellow to off-white, bittersweet, can be alcoholic when fermented
varieties.yield_notes: High with proper cross-pollination, reliable tropical performer

varieties.name: Nonthapuri (Nonthaburi, Kan Yao strain)
varieties.origin: Nonthaburi Province, Thailand - famous for premium durian cultivation including Kan Yao and Mon Thong strains
varieties.traits: Premium quality variety, highly sought-after in Asia, variable size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Superior taste, famous Nonthaburi quality, creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Medium to good, highly valued

varieties.name: Chanee (D123, Gibbon)
varieties.origin: Thailand, established in India since 1980s-1990s
varieties.traits: Distinctive yellow color, thick-fleshed variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick, sweet, buttery yellow, sticky, bittersweet notes
varieties.yield_notes: High, consistent bearer
varieties.availability: State Horticultural Farms, specialty tropical monsoon climates nurseries

varieties.name: Musang King (Mao Shan Wang, D197, Raja Kunyit)
varieties.origin: Malaysia, Pahang/Raub region, discovered 1990s, now world-famous
varieties.traits: Medium size 2-3kg, greenish-yellow ochre husk with color gradient, pyramid-shaped thorns, star pattern at base
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick deep golden-yellow, rich bittersweet taste, creamy smooth slightly dry-sticky texture (liam chooi), perfect balance
varieties.yield_notes: Medium, highly valued, premium prices
varieties.availability: Imported fresh fruit in Lulu hypermarkets tropical monsoon climates, limited nursery availability for saplings, expensive

varieties.name: Sukun (Breadfruit)
varieties.origin: Central Java, Indonesia, named 'breadfruit' for appearance, traditional variety cultivated for generations
varieties.traits: Small round durian, bright yellow flesh, distinctive appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright yellow, sweet with subtle notes, favored by Indonesian durian enthusiasts
varieties.yield_notes: Good, reliable Indonesian cultivar

varieties.name: Petruk (Jepara Specialty)
varieties.origin: Jepara, Central Java, Indonesia - rare heritage variety, named after Javanese puppet character
varieties.traits: Moderately-sized elongated teardrop shape 1-1.5kg, yellow/bronze/green spiky husk, highly prized
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy yogurt-like consistency, ivory-colored, sweet tropical flavor with lemon overtones, funky acidic bitter finish, unique taste profile
varieties.yield_notes: Rare, limited availability, 3x price of regular durian in Java markets
varieties.availability: Extremely rare, specialty collectors only, Jepara region Java

varieties.name: Sitokong (Betawi Traditional)
varieties.origin: Betawi region (Jakarta area), Indonesia, traditional variety, superior national cultivar

varieties.name: Black Thorn (D200, Ochee, Duri Hitam)
varieties.origin: Penang, Malaysia - registered 2015, relatively new premium variety, won 2012 Penang State Durian Competition
varieties.traits: Roundish shape, star pattern at bottom, characteristic black thorn/stamen protruding from base, reddish-brown ridge/valley line in middle
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep burnt orange to rosy orange, thick core, smooth creamy custardy texture, bittersweet taste rivaling Musang King
varieties.yield_notes: Good, growing in popularity, premium prices

varieties.name: Red Prawn (Udang Merah, D175, Ang Hei/Hong Xia)
varieties.origin: Pahang and Johor, Malaysia, registered variety, Penang specialty (unique to Penang)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized oval fruit, brownish-green husk with short thorns, distinctive reddish-orange flesh color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick reddish-orange to orange hue, sticky sweet with milky aftertaste, creamy rich texture, minimal bitterness, voluminous flesh with small seeds, sweet like dessert
varieties.yield_notes: Good, high flesh-to-seed ratio, seasonal summer availability (Penang)

varieties.name: IOI (D168, Johor Mas, D101, Hajah Hasmah)
varieties.origin: Muar, Johor, Malaysia - registered 1989, one of 12 premium Johor varieties, heritage variety
varieties.traits: Medium-large 1.3-2kg (occasionally 3-4kg), roundish slightly elongated oval shape, light green to brown homogeneous husk, dense slim spikes
varieties.flesh_fruit: Medium-thick solid yellow, easily dislodged from seed, sweet premium taste, balanced quality, medium seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - old trees 400-500 fruits, excellent weather resistance especially to rainfall, consistent quality, May-August season

varieties.name: Puangmanee (Puang Manee, Crystal Durian, Phung Manee)
varieties.origin: Chanthaburi, Thailand - mother tree still alive and producing after 70+ years, heritage variety
varieties.traits: Small variety 1.3-1.8kg, oval slightly tapered shape, perfect for single sitting consumption
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright golden color, rich sweet flesh, one of Thailand's sweetest varieties, creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good, smaller fruits allow higher fruit count per tree
varieties.availability: Thai nurseries, exported to Malaysia, China, Australia - potential for tropical monsoon climates import

varieties.name: Chantaburi No. 1 and No. 2 (Chanthaburi Hybrids)
varieties.origin: Thai Department of Agriculture, released 2007-2008, modern breeding program
varieties.traits: Bred for very mild odor, fine texture, early maturity
varieties.flesh_fruit: Mild aroma designed to attract Western consumers, good taste, fine texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good, early bearing

varieties.name: Kampot Durian (Cambodia GI Protected)
varieties.origin: Kampot Province, Cambodia - Geographical Indication (GI) status from European Union, protected variety
varieties.traits: Known for unique characteristics and quality, EU-recognized specialty product
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy texture, delicate sweetness, fragrant aroma, highly regarded quality
varieties.yield_notes: Good in tropical monsoon climate
